payload.answer: Treat this as a status progression/update. The existing state says the Brain Slack agent was planned/open; the new statement says the MVP is now implemented. Commit the implemented MVP state and mark the earlier planned/open state as superseded for current-status purposes rather than keeping both as current.
citations: Existing: The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open.
New: The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented.

entity_resolution.reason: The new statement refers to the same project lineage but a more specific scope (MVP implementation) rather than a distinct unrelated entity.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent MVP is implemented.

details: Detected same project lineage with narrower scope in the new fact.
Classified as a temporal status transition, not a contradiction requiring rejection.
Prepared a durable memory card for the implemented MVP state only.

repair_options: Update the project record to reflect a temporal transition from planned/open to MVP implemented.
If the system tracks granular scope, store implementation status on the MVP sub-entity and avoid asserting the full project is fully complete.
Mark the older planned/open status as historical/superseded, not current.
